definitions.text: A long, thin piece of land; any long, thin area.
examples: The countries were in dispute over the ownership of a strip of desert about 100 metres wide.

definitions.text: A long, thin piece of any material; any such material collectively.
examples: Papier mache is made from strips of paper.
Squeeze a strip of glue along the edge and then press down firmly.

definitions.text: To remove or take away, often in strips or stripes.
examples: Norm will strip the old varnish before painting the chair.
Seeing that no one else was about, he stripped and dived into the river.
In the seedy club, a group of drunken men were watching a woman stripping.

definitions.text: The act of removing one's clothes; a striptease.
examples: She stood up on the table and did a strip.

definitions.text: Denotes a version of a game in which losing players must progressively remove their clothes.
